description:
This image displays a side-by-side comparison of very high resolution (VHR) Earth Observation (EO) data demonstrating correct and incorrect object delineation for Land Cover / Land Use (LC/LU) mapping, specifically in the context of Riparian Zones (RZ). Both panels show underlying reddish-brown and green/yellowish agricultural or vegetated land, with overlaid black polygon boundaries and class labels.

The left panel, marked with a red 'X', illustrates an incorrect delineation. A linear light blue feature (possibly a road or narrow water body) is shown partially integrated within a larger polygon, which is labelled with the land cover class code "1121". Another adjacent polygon is also labelled "1121". A yellow arrow points to the ambiguous boundary where the linear feature is not properly separated from the surrounding polygon.

The right panel, marked with a green checkmark, illustrates the correct delineation. The linear light blue feature, again indicated by a yellow arrow, is now fully separated by distinct polygon boundaries. The polygon above this feature is labelled "1121", while the linear feature itself, or the polygon immediately adjacent to it on the lower side, is labelled with the land cover class code "112". This demonstrates adherence to mapping rules that require distinct linear features to be delineated as separate objects with their own class labels.
